A purpose-built calibration and predictive maintenance platform for industrial sites like this one.

Live product
Registered Australian business
Purpose-built for industrial sites
The Current System

Where calibration breaks down today.

01 · FRAGMENTATION

Records scattered across systems

MEX work orders, L: drive folders, technician inboxes — no centralised source of instrument information.

02 · FIELD BLINDNESS

No tolerance visibility in the plant

Readings handwritten on paper. Pass or fail can't be determined until the technician is back at a desk.

03 · NO TRENDING

No drift analysis or early warning

Instruments are caught when they fail, not predicted to fail. No way to know if an instrument is degrading or out of tolerance until it's out.

04 · CONTRACTOR GAPS

External reports lost in email

Contractor calibrations don't often reach MEX or the L: drive, leaving gaps in the record.

05 · MHF VERIFICATION

Trip-test history hard to confirm

Confirming whether an instrument is within calibration during an MHF trip test requires a trip back to the office to search.

06 · DOUBLE-ENTRY

Transcription errors and wasted time

Print Excel template → fill out in field → calculate error → scan through to emails → save to MEX and L: drive.

The Fix

How CalCheq solves every one of them.

One system. One workflow. Every challenge addressed directly.
01 · FragmentationRecords scattered across MEX, L: drive, and contractor email chains.
→
Centralised registerEvery instrument, every calibration, every certificate in one searchable system.
02 · Field blindnessTolerance unknown until back at a desk.
→
Real-time pass / failPass/fail computed live on your phone against tolerance as readings are entered.
03 · No trendingNo predictive insight — instruments caught failing, not before.
→
Drift analyticsLinear regression on error trend. Projected failure date for every instrument.
04 · Contractor gapsExternal reports lost in email.
→
One workflow for allContractor calibration results saved to CalCheq. Same platform, nothing slips.
05 · MHF verificationTrip-test history means a walk back to the office.
→
Instant lookupLast cal date and result on any phone, in one tap, anywhere in the plant.
06 · Double-entryPaper → scan → Excel → MEX → L: drive.
→
One submissionPDF cert auto-generated, emailed, and archived. Zero re-keying.
Why It Matters

Four reasons this is worth doing.

🛡

Safety

SIS and safety-critical instrument integrity becomes auditable and visible in real time — not buried in a scanned PDF on a shared drive.

Drift is caught weeks before tolerance is breached — before a SIS trip risks people or plant.
✓

Compliance

ISO/IEC 17025, IEC 61511-1 and AS ISO 9001 traceability are built into every record. Audit-ready by default — not as a scramble.

Full calibration history on-screen in seconds during any audit or MHF verification.
$

Cost

Eliminates double-entry, wasted return trips to the desk, and the after-hours call-ins caused by failed or out-of-tolerance instruments.

Minutes saved per calibration × hundreds of instruments per year = measurable labour return.
⚙

Plant Integrity

Catch instrument drift before it causes a trip. Schedule preventative work proactively based on instrument health, process criticality, and predictive analysis.

Planned work replaces reactive work. Fewer unplanned shutdowns. Higher availability.
The Platform

A guided live tour.

Open the live platform ↗
calcheq.com
Smart Analytics

Know before it fails.

Three lenses on instrument health — click a tab to explore
Recommendations
Drift Alerts
Repeat Failures
Recommendations

Rule-based insights, surfaced automatically.

CalCheq analyses every instrument's calibration history and surfaces actionable recommendations — before they become problems.

  • Extend interval — instruments consistently well inside tolerance can have their interval extended to save labour.
  • Shorten interval — instruments drifting fast need more frequent checks.
  • Flag for replacement — repeat failures or unrecoverable drift → candidate for replacement.
  • Procedure review — outlier results may indicate a flawed procedure.
Drift Alerts

Linear regression on every instrument's error trend.

CalCheq plots as-found error over time, calculates drift rate, and projects the date each instrument is expected to exceed tolerance. Act before it fails — not after.

  • Sparkline trend for every instrument at a glance · Projected failure date from historical drift.
  • Sorted worst-first so the riskiest assets surface to the top · Full drift chart with tolerance band and projection on any instrument detail page.
Repeat Failures

Bad actors surfaced automatically.

Instruments that fail repeatedly get captured and filtered into one view — so you can see your problem assets at a glance and deal with them structurally, not one work-order at a time.

  • Ranked by failure count — worst offenders first.
  • Filtered by area or criticality — focus where it matters.
  • Direct link to history — full audit trail in one click.
  • Trigger replacement decisions with evidence, not guesswork.
Reporting

Done. Certified. Sent.

Generated and emailed automatically on completion
Single Calibration Certificate

One submission. Certified. Emailed. Archived.

PDF calibration certificates are automatically generated and emailed to the technician and supervisors the moment a calibration is submitted and approved — no scanning, no re-typing, no attaching to MEX manually.

  • Auto-named {tag}_{date}.pdf for instant filing.
  • Full traceability — ISO/IEC 17025, IEC 61511, AS ISO 9001 references baked in.
  • Pass / fail calculated and printed — never ambiguous.
  • Single source of truth — the same PDF everyone receives.
Historical Calibration Certificate

The full story. On demand.

Multi-page historical PDF calibration reports can be generated automatically for any instrument — outlining the entire calibration history, performance summary, and as-found error trends over time.

  • Performance summary — pass rate, marginal and fail counts at a glance.
  • Error trend chart — as-found drift plotted across every historical calibration.
  • Full record table — every calibration with cert ref, technician, and result.
  • One click · MHF-ready — hand to an auditor without reformatting.
Technician submits → Pass/fail calculated → PDF auto-generated, emailed, archived → Audit trail recorded.
Implementation

A clear, low-risk path to day-one value.

Three phases. No big-bang migration. No disruption to MEX.

1
Week 1

Setup & Historical Import

  • IT data-governance approval
  • 255 historical calibration PDFs converted & imported
  • Instrument register populated from the L: drive
  • Dashboard populated from day one
  • Drift trends live for every instrument with history
2
Months 1 – 6

Live Use & Monitoring

  • All new calibrations recorded in CalCheq
  • I personally support setup & onboarding
  • Daily real-world validation on your floor
  • Team feedback continuously folded in
  • No change to MEX — runs alongside
3
End of Trial

Review & Decision

  • Ease of use — did it help the team?
  • Information quality — better than today?
  • Suitability — does it fit IXOM's workflow?
  • Consider subscription & rollout to other sites
  • If it doesn't fit, nothing lost
